meaning: adj.奥尔德沃文化的(东非旧石器时代的文化之一,以简单的石制砍砸器、刮削器为特征)
examples: These tools were useful for a wider range of tasks than the Oldowan ones.
The style of stone tools called the Oldowan appeared 2.5 million years ago and stayed unchanged for more than a million years.
On the northwest shoreline of Kenya's Lake Turkana, at a site known as Kokiselei, researchers previously found both Oldowan and Acheulian tool types in the same 1.76-million-year-old sediments.
